Claim: C1Q is a macrophage/complement traceability signal in NSCLC.
Primary data/resource: TCGA/GEO transcriptomics; spatial transcriptomics; public proteomes
Analysis: Transparent C1Q-only score, spatial autocorrelation, public proteomic marker correlations
Source-data file: data/S1_source_data_and_machine_readable_tables.zip; supplementary/supplementary_material.pdf; figures/Figure6.pdf for selected module context
Allowed conclusion: C1Q-associated signals are reproducibly traceable across public data layers.
Boundary / not claimed: Not a tumor-specific marker, validated biomarker, or clinical prediction tool.

Claim: C1Q-only survival traceability is weak and heterogeneous.
Manuscript anchor: Figure 5; C1Q-Only and Fuller Traceability Scores
Allowed conclusion: The weak signal argues for biological context rather than clinical deployment.
Boundary / not claimed: No robust standalone prognostic test is claimed.

Claim: The fuller traceability score summarizes retrospective signal behavior.
Analysis: Retrospective signal-behavior summary with AUC and C-index
Allowed conclusion: The fuller score characterizes a multi-feature immune-context program.
Boundary / not claimed: No locked clinical threshold, treatment guidance, or prospective validation is claimed.

Claim: C1Q and SPP1 represent related but non-equivalent spatial myeloid contexts.
Primary data/resource: 10x Visium and spatial transcriptomic support files
Analysis: Moran's I, hotspot-like summaries, immune-context correlations
Manuscript anchor: Figure 3; Spatial and Proteomic Evidence for C1Q/SPP1 Context
Allowed conclusion: C1Q is more macrophage/complement traceable; SPP1 is more tumor-neighborhood enriched.
Boundary / not claimed: No binary C1Q-versus-SPP1 mechanism or causal antagonism is claimed.

Claim: SPP1-high and C1Q/SPP1 dual-high macrophages occupy tumor-neighborhood enriched and relatively CD8-lower contexts.
Analysis: Macrophage-restricted module assignment; kNN100 tumor/CD8 neighborhood summaries; paired sample-level contrasts
Manuscript anchor: Figure 6; Selected Official CosMx Macrophage-Module Evidence
Allowed conclusion: Selected spatial single-cell evidence supports a macrophage-context hypothesis.
Boundary / not claimed: No causal CD8 exclusion, immune suppression, cell-cell signaling, or treatment resistance is claimed.

Claim: Independent 2024 scRNA/Visium resources support contextual plausibility.
Analysis: Patient-level module summaries, label summaries, and external context checks
Allowed conclusion: The C1Q/SPP1 macrophage-module concept is directionally plausible outside the original CosMx material.
Boundary / not claimed: Not formal independent clinical validation and not a rebuilt model.

Claim: The overall contribution is a reproducible cancer-informatics framework.
Primary data/resource: All source datasets and generated source-data files
Analysis: Source-data manifest, supplementary methods, figure-source mapping, and conservative inference boundaries
Manuscript anchor: Data Availability; Code Availability; Supplementary Methods
Allowed conclusion: The study provides a reproducible hypothesis-generation framework for future validation.
Boundary / not claimed: Not a completed mechanism study, clinical assay, or therapeutic-target validation.
